body.title: callmap.py — reverse callgraph for designated API functions; audit-check-wired
body.result: Shipped commit ee03c1a5. callmap.py + lab/docs/callmap.md (10458 lines, 741 APIs, 6410 caller refs) + 13 unit tests + audit-check `callmap-fresh` registered. Performance: 4.2s full regen (was timing out >120s before single-AST-walk refactor). 6 of 6 audit checks PASS. Suite 4326 → 4339. Unblocks T-cc-queue-write-race — that migration now uses the callmap as planning input.
body.description:
Akien (2026-05-03): once upon a time we had an architecture doc + implementation map. Docs-in-code rollout handles the forward axis (concept → location) already via top-of-file canonical docstrings. The reverse axis (function → who calls it) is missing. A callmap would have made today's 'find every queue.json reader' a 3-second query instead of a grep+eyeball pass.

Filed as the planning input for T-cc-queue-write-race (now gated on this ticket).

**API marking convention:**
- Default: all top-level non-_ functions and class methods in `lab/claudecode/*.py` + `wild_igor/igor/tools/*.py` count as API
- Override per-module: `__api__ = ['name1', 'name2']` module-level list for explicit narrowing
- Or `# API: <one-line>` comment immediately above the function for richer descriptions

**Caller detection:**
1. Direct calls — `from mod import f; f(...)` and `import mod; mod.f(...)` resolved via AST + import graph
2. Subprocess invocations — `subprocess.run([..., 'cc_queue.py', ...])` detected by string-matching the script path. Important because pe_chain calls cc_queue via subprocess, not import.
3. os.system / os.popen / Popen — same string-match shape

**Output:**
- `lab/docs/callmap.md` — one section per module → API → caller list (file:line + brief context)
- Sorted: APIs alphabetical within module; callers alphabetical within API
- Generated, committed to git, refreshed by audit

**Wire as audit check:** add to audit_runner.py registered checks. Failure mode: regenerating produces a different file than the committed copy (drift detection).

**Affected files:**
- `lab/claudecode/callmap.py` (NEW — tool + AST walker + emitter)
- `tests/test_callmap.py` (NEW — unit tests for AST parsing, subprocess pattern, output format)
- `lab/docs/callmap.md` (NEW — generated output, committed)
- Audit registration via `lab/claudecode/audit_add.py` add forever

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (callmap.py needs canonical top-of-file docstring); test-plan-required; audit-as-staleness-detector.

**Scope boundary:** IN — AST-based caller detection (direct + subprocess), markdown output, audit-check wiring, tests on a fixture + smoke test on the real repo. OUT — dynamic dispatch resolution (getattr-style — note as known blind spot in tool docstring); palace integration (markdown only for now; promote later if it earns the upgrade); type analysis; cross-repo callmap (TheIgors only).

**Test plan:**
- Unit: parse a 3-file fixture, assert API functions identified per convention
- Unit: assert direct callers found (intra-module, cross-module, dotted access)
- Unit: assert subprocess invocations matched by script path string
- Smoke: run on the real repo, assert known callers appear (e.g., `cc_queue._load` has callers in `cmd_done`/`cmd_claim`/etc.; `cc_queue.py` script-path appears in pe_chain subprocess calls)
- Audit: re-running the tool with no code changes produces an identical file (idempotent)

**Why now:** about to migrate 25+ queue.json readers in T-cc-queue-write-race; pre-built callmap = trivial planning input + verification (after migration, callmap should show the new uses). Generally useful for any future broad refactor.
